name: rbac_permissions_v1
description: RBAC permissions matrix training dataset for adapterOS K-sparse LoRA routing system. Covers the 5-role hierarchy (Admin > Operator > SRE > Compliance > Viewer) with 20+ permissions, permission inheritance chains, privilege escalation detection, least-privilege assignment, multi-tenant scoping, and permission boundary edge cases. 2000 examples with detailed reasoning about why access is granted or denied.

entries.notes: 2000 examples: 400 permission checks, 300 role assignment scenarios, 400 privilege escalation attempts (negative), 300 least-privilege patterns, 300 multi-tenant RBAC, 300 permission boundaries. Shuffled with deterministic seed.

provenance.review_notes: Synthetic dataset generated from the adapterOS RBAC permission matrix. All permission checks validated against the 5-role hierarchy with inheritance. Privilege escalation examples include audit trail and alert behavior. Multi-tenant examples enforce strict tenant isolation boundaries.

evaluation_gates: Permission checks correctly map each permission to its minimum required role
Role inheritance is consistent: every permission available to role R is available to all roles above R
Privilege escalation attempts always result in denial with audit log entry and alert
Shared permissions (AdapterList, AdapterView, TrainingView, PolicyView, MetricsView) are accessible by all 5 roles
Admin-exclusive permissions (AdapterDelete, PolicySign, TenantManage, NodeManage, SystemConfig) are denied for all non-Admin roles
Multi-tenant examples enforce strict tenant isolation: Admin of tenant-a cannot access tenant-b resources
Cross-tenant access requires SystemAdmin role, never tenant-scoped Admin
Least-privilege recommendations never suggest a higher role than necessary
SRE role cannot modify adapters or training state in any example
Compliance role cannot execute inference or modify any resource in any example
All negative-label examples describe denied operations with correct error codes and audit behavior
Permission boundary edge cases correctly distinguish between similar-sounding permissions across roles

quality_thresholds.notes: Security-critical dataset: quality floor is higher than behavioral datasets because incorrect RBAC training could teach the model to hallucinate permissions that don't exist.

intent: Train the adapter to reason precisely about adapterOS RBAC: which roles hold which permissions, how inheritance works, why privilege escalation is blocked, how to assign least-privilege roles for operational scenarios, how multi-tenant isolation works, and where permission boundaries create surprising but correct access decisions.

permission_checks.description: Direct permission check questions: Can role X perform action Y?

role_assignment.description: Scenario-based role selection: given requirements, which role fits?

privilege_escalation.description: Attempts to use permissions above role level. All negative label.

least_privilege.description: Designing minimal permission sets for operational needs.

permission_boundaries.description: Edge cases where permission boundaries create non-obvious decisions.

role_hierarchy.description: Each role inherits all permissions of roles below it in the hierarchy.

multi_tenancy.shared_resources: require SystemAdmin or explicit cross-tenant grant

enforcement.denial_response: 403 Forbidden with error body containing required_permission and current_role
enforcement.audit_logging: All denied requests logged to immutable audit trail with timestamp, principal, action, resource, and denial_reason
enforcement.alert_on_escalation: Repeated denied attempts trigger security alert to tenant Admin and SystemAdmin
